{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 -->
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 =
|家祖 = Unknown
|種別 = Unknown
|出身地 = Unknown
|根拠地 = Unknown
|人物 =
|支流 =
}}
'''Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''suwa no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''suwa kokuzō'') is a fictitious {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} whose name appears only in the {{ill|forged book|ja|偽書}} ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi Taiseikyō|ja|先代旧事本紀大成経}}''.

No records of the Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko exist other than the Taiseikyō and works influenced by it, and it is considered a miswriting of the {{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造}}.

== Overview ==
=== Writing ===
In the Kokuzō Hongi of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi Taiseikyō|ja|先代旧事本紀大成経}}'', the entry '''Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' appears, and no Suwa no Kuni no Miyatsuko is seen in any sources other than works influenced by the Taiseikyō<ref name="名前なし-20231105142147">Maeda Ryōtarō, "Reconsideration of the Taihōke-bon 'Shin-shi Keizu' and 'Aso-ke Ryaku Keifu'—The Reconstructed Suwa Traditions," ''Kokugo Kokubun Kenkyū'' No. 161 (Hokkaido University Kokubungakkai, August 2023)</ref>.

=== Existence ===
{{ill|Niino Naokichi|ja|新野直吉}} argued that "regardless of the written form, no Suwa Kuni no Miyatsuko existed," and Satō Yūichi concluded that "it was one clan of the {{ill|Shinanu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|科野国造}} who had built pseudo-kinship relations"<ref>Satō Yūichi, "Clans and Beliefs of Ancient Shinano" (2021, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan)</ref>.

=== Ancestry ===
* In the traditions of the Suwa Shrine Naka-sha enshrined in {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市|lt=Hokuto City|lt_ja=北杜市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, and the ''Kai Kokushi'', during the era of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|lt=Emperor Sujin|lt_ja=崇神朝}}, {{ill|Take-Nunakawa-Wake-no-Mikoto|ja|建沼河別命}} was enfeoffed in the western region, and his grandson Ōomi-no-Mikoto was appointed Suha no Kuni no Miyatsuko. However, this is an error for the "'''{{ill|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|那須国造}}''' Ōomi-no-Mikoto" found in the Kokuzō Hongi of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}''<ref name="名前なし-20231105142147"/>.

== Base ==
The construction of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} in Shinanu Province centered on the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} basin in northern Shinano (the territory of the {{ill|Shinanu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|科野国造}}), including the {{ill|Hanishina Kofun Group|ja|埴科古墳群}} beginning from the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, and on the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} basin in southern Shinano (south of {{ill|Takamori, Nagano|ja|高森町 (長野県)|lt=Takamori Town|lt_ja=高森町}}), including the {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} beginning from the latter half of the 5th century. Therefore, the possibility that a kuni no miyatsuko was placed in Suwa is denied<ref>{{ill|Satō Yūichi|ja|佐藤雄一}}, ''Shinano no Kokuzō Seiryoku''</ref>.

=== Territory ===
After the ritsuryō system, {{ill|Suwa Province|ja|諏訪国}} was temporarily separated from Shinano Province, and this is considered the territory of the former Suwa<ref>Satō Yūichi, ''Shinano no Kokuzō Seiryoku''</ref>.

From the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} through the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, Shinanu was the Chikuma River basin centered on Sarashina and Hanishina, and the center of Shinanu ({{ill|Shinanu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|科野国造}}) is considered to have been the area from present-day {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|ja|千曲市|lt=Chikuma City|lt_ja=千曲市}} (former {{ill|Kōshoku|ja|更埴市}}) where the Hanishina Kofun Group — led by the {{ill|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|ja|森将軍塚古墳}}, the largest {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} in the prefecture and thought to be the tomb of the great king of Shinanu — is preserved, to the southern part of {{ill|Nagano (city)|ja|長野市|lt=Nagano City|lt_ja=長野市}} (former {{ill|Sarashina District|ja|更級郡}}) where the {{ill|Kawayanagi-Shōgunzuka Kofun|ja|川柳将軍塚古墳}} is located<ref>Fukushima Masaki, "The Formation of Shinano Province" in Furukawa Sadao, Fukushima Masaki, Ihara Kesao, Aoki Toshiyuki, Kobira Chifumi, ''Nagano-ken no Rekishi'', 2003, p. 43</ref>. On the other hand, the only keyhole-shaped kofun constructed in the Suwa region is the {{ill|Shimosuwa-Aozuka Kofun|ja|下諏訪青塚古墳}} in {{ill|Shimosuwa, Nagano|ja|下諏訪町|lt=Shimosuwa Town|lt_ja=下諏訪町}}, and this kofun is thought to be the okutsuki (burial place) of the {{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏}} (same lineage as the {{ill|Ō clan|ja|多氏}}), the Ōhōri family that venerated the Shimo-sha{{efn|There is a theory that the Sugumo-zuka Kofun and Futago-zuka Kofun are keyhole-shaped kofun, but the tumulus destruction is severe, and details are unclear.}}.

== Footnotes ==
=== Notes ===
{{Notelist}}
=== Citations ===
{{Reflist|refs=
<!--
<ref name="宝賀寿男著「諏訪社の奉斎氏族」『古樹紀之房間』、2010年。">宝賀寿男著「諏訪社の奉斎氏族」『古樹紀之房間』、2010年。</ref>
-->
}}{{デフォルトソート:すわのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:架空の国家を舞台とした作品]]
[[Category:偽作]]
[[Category:長野県の歴史|偽すわのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:信濃国|偽すわのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:洲羽国造]]
